description: A phrase with 'theli' applies its statement to the members one by one: each person, each day. 'theula' (UNIV) gathers the complete set, while 'theli' keeps its members individually in view. Both cover the same members; one speaks collectively and the other distributively.
articulatory_notes: Extend the tip slightly past the teeth and blow without voice for 'th', then retract it for front, middle-height 'e'. Make 'l' by contacting the alveolar area while air escapes at the sides; raise the tongue into 'i' to finish. Stress falls on 'the'.
sound_symbolism: The shared opening lets a learner hear 'theli' beside 'theula' (UNIV) as a related universal form. Its quick close on high 'i' creates a light stepping rhythm, one member after another.
usage_notes: The noun after 'theli' stays bare because the distributive already supplies quantity. Its predicate applies separately to every member under discussion.

examples.phi: theli phemi ta themo noru howela.
